tldr.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
tldr.author: maltalex
bullets: S&P 500 eligibility rules block private AI giants OpenAI and Anthropic, restricting passive public investment and keeping them reliant on VC/hyperscalers.
OpenAIвЂ™s capped-profit structure and AnthropicвЂ™s public benefit charter conflict with public market index standards, preventing direct retail index exposure.
Exclusion forces leading AI labs to seek massive capital through private rounds, deepening dependence on Microsoft, Amazon, and Google.

tldr.title: A Little Explanation of Little's Law
tldr.author: ugur
bullets: Little's Law ($L = \lambda \times W$) relates concurrency ($L$), throughput (\lambda), and latency ($W$) in stable queueing systems.
Engineers use it to size thread pools, set connection limits, and plan capacity without needing complex probability distribution models.
Increasing throughput without reducing processing time strictly requires scaling system concurrency and resource capacity proportionally.

tldr.title: The Smart TV in Your LivingRoom Is a Node in the AIScraping Economy
tldr.author: gerikson
bullets: Smart TVs running Android TV or Tizen are integrated into residential proxy networks like Bright Data via embedded app SDKs.
AI firms leverage these residential IPs to bypass anti-scraping blocks, masking automated scraping bots as legitimate home user traffic.
Enterprise defenders must expect sophisticated scraping traffic originating from residential IP ranges, complicating traditional IP-based reputation filtering.

tldr.title: Cloudflare CEO is lying to you about the bot traffic jump
tldr.author: speckx
bullets: Cloudflare allegedly inflated bot traffic metrics by reclassifying legitimate API requests and system monitoring tools as automated threats to upsell services.
Relying blindly on vendor WAF telemetry can trigger false positives, blocking legitimate automated DevOps workflows, health checks, and API integrations.
Deploy self-hosted telemetry like Prometheus or Envoy logs to verify actual bot-to-user traffic ratios independently of CDN-provided dashboards.

tldr.title: SpaceX, Other Mega IPOs Denied Fast Index Entry by S&P
tldr.author: tristanj
bullets: S&P rules deny mega IPOs like SpaceX fast-track index inclusion, delaying passive fund inflows and immediate market cap boosts.
Delayed indexation impacts liquidity and valuation timelines for late-stage startups, directly affecting employee equity and RSU liquidation strategies.
This policy forces active asset managers to set initial prices, preventing major ETFs from automatically purchasing shares at debut.
